summary:
The infant likely has Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome given his acute onset of severe respiratory distress post-discharge followed by signs suggestive of cardiogenic shock due to compromised left-sided cardiac output. Urgent evaluation including detailed echocardiogram must also rule out alternatives such as Ebstein anomaly despite its lower likelihood based on presented data but maintaining a broad differential ensures optimal care planning moving forward.
The neonate probably suffers from Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome characterized by sudden respiratory issues after being well at home coupled with indications pointing towards cardiogenic shock because of impaired blood flow through one side of heart. Essential diagnostic steps involve conducting an urgent comprehensive echo-cardiographic assessment while keeping open possibilities like Ebstein anomalies although they are rarer scenarios fitting this case presentation partially. Summary:

A newborn presenting with severe respiratory distress and shock shortly after seemingly normal hospital discharge strongly suggests Hypoplastic Left Heart Syndrome. Immediate thorough echocardiography is essential alongside consideration of alternate conditions like Ebstein anomaly until definitive results clarify the primary cause. 

This summary captures the critical aspects focusing directly on the most plausible condition supported by symptomatology along with necessary next-steps highlighted appropriately within two concise statements aligned strictly according your instructions provided earlier regarding length constraints and emphasis areas specified clearly above.
